mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are positive in >65% of high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.2 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and rising real short-term interest rates indicates tightening financial conditions that suppress aggregate demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant or forward returns remain positive in >55% of divergence periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects persistent supply chain bottlenecks that constrain corporate output growth, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to positive or Rank IC falls below 0.02 during post-shock recovery windows in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 3.0 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of high-inflation trigger months or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands consumer spending capacity and supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >50% of high-growth payroll months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm strengthening domestic demand that lifts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to outperform baseline or forward returns are negative in >55% of co-movement periods during early-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 100 index baseline indicates a contraction in manufacturing activity that reduces corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of sub-100 observations or the threshold signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward testing.
